Name: pac_bayes_layer_skipping
Title: PAC-Bayes Frameworks for Latency-Critical Inference with Jacobian-Based Layer Skipping
Short Hypothesis: By integrating PAC-Bayes frameworks with Jacobian-based layer-skipping, we can derive strictly bounded-overhead protocols for latency-critical inference in deep learning, ensuring empirical validation, mechanistic transparency, and reproducibility.
Related Work: Existing work on PAC-Bayes frameworks and layer-skipping techniques focuses on separate aspects of the problem. Our proposal combines these approaches to provide a comprehensive solution for latency-critical inference, distinguishing itself from previous work by prioritizing empirical validation, mechanistic transparency, and reproducibility.
Abstract: We propose a novel approach to latency-critical inference in deep learning by integrating PAC-Bayes frameworks with Jacobian-based layer-skipping. Our method provides strictly bounded-overhead protocols for inference, ensuring empirical validation, mechanistic transparency, and reproducibility. We evaluate our approach using first-class latency benchmarks and explicit perturbation-cost and memory-bandwidth accounting, targeting throughput-accuracy Pareto frontiers.
Experiments: Evaluate the proposed approach on benchmark datasets (e.g., ImageNet, CIFAR-10) using various deep learning models (e.g., ResNet, MobileNet)
Compare the performance of our approach with state-of-the-art methods for latency-critical inference
Conduct ablation studies to analyze the effect of different components of our approach (e.g., PAC-Bayes frameworks, Jacobian-based layer-skipping) on the overall performance
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed approach may require significant computational resources for training and evaluation
The integration of PAC-Bayes frameworks with Jacobian-based layer-skipping may introduce additional complexity to the model
The approach may not be suitable for all types of deep learning models or applications
